title: Work Session Procedure
organization: Two Marshalls Studios — Studio Operating System (TMS-OS)
summary: Defines the controlled process for starting, executing, verifying, documenting, and closing studio work sessions during the WS086 documentation reconciliation phase.

sections.title: Purpose
paragraphs: This procedure defines how a Two Marshalls Studios work session is started, controlled, documented, verified, and closed.
Its purpose is to make studio work repeatable, recoverable, and understandable across computers, locations, future chats, and future work sessions.
A work session must preserve the agreed studio vision, the verified current state, the active milestone, and the exact next action.

sections.title: Scope
paragraphs: This procedure applies to work performed on:
items: game projects, including Dead Roads and approved future projects;
development, assets, publishing, marketing, and business operations;
historical recovery, migration, reconciliation, and verification work.

sections.title: Source-of-Truth Rule
paragraphs: The approved operating model is:
TMS-OS / StudioDB = Structured source of truth.
Studio Portal = Governed presentation layer.
Google Headquarters = Human-readable documentation layer (when used).
Chat = Collaborative working environment.
Repository reconciliation and documentation updates must remain synchronized.

sections.title: Work Session Naming
paragraphs: Each controlled work session must have a unique work session ID using the approved WS numbering sequence.
Examples:
Historical letter suffixes must be preserved when they are part of the verified record. Missing or uncertain history must not be invented.

sections.title: Required Start-of-Session Check
paragraphs: Before new work begins, verify the following:
If the current state cannot be verified, the session must remain in recovery or reconciliation mode until the uncertainty is resolved or explicitly recorded as unresolved.
items: known blockers, risks, or unresolved history.

sections.title: Work Session Opening Procedure
items: Open the current human-readable state record.
Confirm the active project and milestone.
Confirm the current work session ID.
Confirm that the required files and working environment are available.

sections.title: Focus and Change-Control Rule
paragraphs: During a build session, work must remain strictly focused on the agreed studio vision and the approved current task.
Do not add unrelated systems, features, redesigns, or enhancements during the session unless they are explicitly approved.
Enhancement ideas that are not part of the approved task must be recorded for later review rather than inserted into the active build.

sections.title: Step-by-Step Execution Rule
paragraphs: Work must be performed in controlled steps.
items: Give one clear step or a tightly bounded group of actions.
State exactly where the action occurs.
State exactly what to click, enter, move, rename, create, or test.
Use exact file paths, names, IDs, dimensions, values, and expected results when applicable.
When code changes are required, provide the complete updated file rather than partial fragments.
Stop for verification at meaningful checkpoints before continuing.

sections.title: Verification Rule
paragraphs: A step is not complete merely because an action was attempted. The result must be checked.
Verification may include:
If a test fails, correct the failure before advancing unless the failure is deliberately recorded as an unresolved blocker.
items: comparison against the last verified stable checkpoint.

sections.title: Stable Checkpoint Rule
paragraphs: After a meaningful body of work passes verification, record a stable checkpoint.
A stable checkpoint should identify:
items: the current architecture or state if it changed;

sections.title: Documentation Update Rule
paragraphs: Documentation must describe the verified result, not an intended result that has not yet been proven.
When required, update:
items: WS-HIST-001, MILE-HIST-001, and DEC-LOG-001 when affected.

sections.title: Multi-Location and Workstation Rule
paragraphs: Important Two Marshalls Studios work must remain accessible from multiple locations and workstations.
No critical studio state, procedure, decision, or recovery information should exist only on one computer.
Permanent human-readable material belongs in the approved Google Headquarters structure. Structured system knowledge remains governed by TMS-OS and StudioDB.

sections.title: Error and Recovery Procedure
items: Capture the exact error, unexpected result, or missing information.
Compare the result with the last verified checkpoint.
Fix only the confirmed issue unless a broader change is approved.
Record unresolved uncertainty rather than filling gaps with assumptions.
Continue only after the result is verified or formally documented as blocked.

sections.title: Work Session Closing Procedure
paragraphs: Before ending a work session:
items: confirm what was tested and passed;
record any failed or unresolved items;
update required structured and human-readable records;
confirm important work is accessible from more than one workstation or location.

sections.title: Required End-of-Session Record
paragraphs: A closed or paused work session should leave behind enough information to answer:
items: What is the exact next step?

sections.title: Prohibited Practices
paragraphs: The following practices are not allowed in a controlled studio work session:
items: silently changing the agreed studio vision;
treating chat as the only permanent record;
leaving critical work accessible from only one computer;
replacing complete code files with unexplained partial snippets when a file change is required;
declaring a result complete before the expected result is confirmed.

sections.title: Procedure Validation
paragraphs: PROC-001 is governed through the repository reconciliation process.
The procedure is considered validated when it renders correctly in the Studio Portal and reflects the approved workflow.

sections.title: Procedure Summary
paragraphs: DOCUMENT:
PROC-001 - Work Session Procedure
SYSTEM:
TMS-OS
PURPOSE:
Control the opening, execution, verification, documentation, and closing of studio work sessions
SOURCE-OF-TRUTH MODEL:
TMS-OS / StudioDB = Structured Source of Truth; Google Workspace = Human-Readable Documentation Layer; Chat = Working Environment
CORE EXECUTION RULE:
Work step by step, do not skip steps, and verify meaningful results before advancing
CHANGE-CONTROL RULE:
Stay focused on the approved studio vision and current task; defer unapproved enhancements
ACCESS RULE:
Important studio work must be accessible from multiple locations and workstations
CURRENT TEST:
First permanent procedure population test
PASS CONDITION:
Final formatted native Google Doc verified in the correct Headquarters folder

revisionHistory.summary: Connected the verified permanent document to the shared structured Document Template Engine.
Reconciled work session procedure with current repository governance and documentation reconciliation workflow.

verification.method: Reconciled against the verified repository during WS086.
